mechanism: Central bank credibility determines whether inflation expectations are anchored, which is the primary discriminant between the low-passthrough regime (0.1-0.3pp) and high-passthrough regime (0.5-0.8pp). Credibility is the strongest cross-sectional predictor of passthrough outcomes.
regime_dependence: Relationship holds universally but the magnitude of credibility's effect is strongest when commodity shocks are large enough to potentially de-anchor expectations. Small shocks are absorbed regardless of credibility.
